title: Payment processor sends duplicate charges on retry
description: When a payment request times out and the client retries, the payment processor sometimes charges the customer twice. This is a data-integrity bug affecting roughly 0.3% of transactions.

triageDetail.reasoning: Confirmed by payment processor webhook logs — two charge events recorded 8 seconds apart for same order ID

reproduced.notes:
Steps:
1. Submit payment request with network timeout set to 100ms
2. Client retries same payment within 2 seconds
Observed: Two charges appear in Stripe dashboard for same order
Expected: Only one charge; second attempt should be recognised as duplicate

uatScenarios.summary: Happy path: successful payment submission and confirmation
Payment declined: card rejected by gateway

kbEntries.summary: ADR for selecting Stripe as the payment gateway

plan.summary:
## Summary
Introduce idempotency keys on all outbound payment requests so the Stripe API deduplicates retries server-side. Add an idempotency key derived from the order ID and attempt number to every PaymentGatewayClient request.

## Documentation impact
docs/runbook/payment-incidents.md — add section on idempotency key troubleshooting

## UAT coverage
New scenarios needed — see Phase 6

## PR
https://github.com/example/myapp/pull/887
plan.dddAnalysis: PaymentIntent aggregate root. Idempotency key is a Value Object (IdempotencyKey) derived from orderId + attemptNumber. PaymentGatewayClient is an infrastructure port — the key is generated in the domain and passed through the application service.
plan.testStrategy: Unit tests for IdempotencyKey VO (collision resistance, format). Integration test for PaymentService that stubs Stripe to verify key is included on every call.

steps.description: Add IdempotencyKey value object to domain/payments/
Update PaymentIntent to generate and expose IdempotencyKey
steps.risks: PaymentIntent is the aggregate root — must preserve existing invariants

steps.description: Thread IdempotencyKey through PaymentService and PaymentGatewayClient
steps.risks: Existing callers of PaymentService pass no idempotency key — need to verify no backwards-compat breakage

potentialChallenges: Idempotency key uniqueness across retries vs across different orders
Clock skew if key includes a timestamp component
Stripe idempotency key TTL (24 hours) — keys older than that won't deduplicate

findings.description: Plan derives idempotency key from orderId only — if customer retries with a different payment method for the same order, the key collision will correctly block the retry at Stripe, but the customer will see a confusing error. The key should include attempt context to distinguish intentional retries from duplicates.
IdempotencyKey.ts should validate that attemptNumber is a positive integer, not just any number.
Consider whether idempotency keys should be logged — they contain order IDs which may be considered PII in some jurisdictions.
No test covers the 24-hour Stripe TTL edge case — if a key expires and the customer retries, the duplicate protection breaks silently.
PaymentGatewayClient now silently drops idempotency keys longer than 255 chars — this truncation should be documented or at least logged as a warning.

resolutions.MED: IdempotencyKey validation: Fixed — added positive-integer guard in IdempotencyKey constructor
resolutions.LOW: PII logging concern: Accepted — idempotency keys do not appear in application logs per current log configuration
resolutions.HIGH: Stripe TTL edge case: Fixed — added TTL-aware test using MockClock; documented 24h limit in PaymentGatewayClient JSDoc
resolutions.MED: truncation undocumented: Fixed — added console.warn when key is truncated
